sentence_reviews.text: Object stores, distributed filesystems, shards, and checkpoint formats persist datasets, weights, optimizer state, embeddings, traces, and artifacts.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports distributed storage, sharding/partitioning, and persistence; deep learning references support model parameters and optimizer state but not the full artifact list in one place.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is plausible as ML infrastructure synthesis; exact object-store/checkpoint-format details need a checkpointing or MLOps source.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Object storage, sharding & checkpoints is modeled as depending on Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as Workflow, cluster & job orchestration, Model servers & inference runtimes, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
